title: ophrys ferrum-equinum subsp. spruneri
intro: Ophrys ferrum-equinum subsp. spruneri, also known as the Mediterranean bee orchid, is a subspecies of the Ophrys ferrum-equinum orchid, a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of this plant are not well-documented, but it is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of the Mediterranean bee orchid include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. However, the plant's medicinal properties and health benefits are not extensively studied, and it is not commonly used in herbal preparations, which typically include teas, infusions, and tinctures.
